Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-83-222 
Mr. Stern has asked that I respond to your request for 
assistance regarding two Amador County Supervisors who may 
become involved as directors and owners of a local bank. 
You 
asked that we send to you previous advice rendered in similar 
circumstances. 
Therefore, I am sending to you a copy of our 
Advice Letter, A-82-039 which deals with similar circumstances. 
Once you know more detail about the facts of your 
supervisors' situations you may wish to seek specific advice 
from us, particularly if either of them will have a 10% or 
greater ownership interest. 
Please do not hesitate to call me 
if you have any questions.

part in the

it seems to

the Board

In a 
1 county such as Amador County, with a 
population of approximately 20,000 people, and no other locally 
owned banks (although there are branches of many state-wide 
banks) f it appears inevitable that eventually either a 
stockholder or a customer of the bank will make some request to

The question is whether or not such a

those two members of the Board of Supervisors who are also

sections.question: Whether two Amador County Supervisors who may become involved as directors and owners of a local bank would face conflicts of interest, particularly if a bank stockholder or customer seeks permits or other entitlements from the Board of Supervisors.
sections.conclusion: The FPPC response directs the requester to a previous advice letter (A-82-039) dealing with similar circumstances and suggests seeking specific advice once more details are known, particularly if either supervisor will have 10% or greater ownership interest.
sections.question_synthetic: Do county supervisors who are directors and stockholders in a newly organized local bank face potential conflicts of interest if the bank itself, its other stockholders, or its customers seek entitlements from the Board of Supervisors?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not provide a direct conclusion but references prior advice letter A-82-039 as guidance for similar situations and recommends seeking specific advice with more detailed facts, especially regarding ownership thresholds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a two-part document consisting of the incoming request from County Counsel (dated September 23, 1983) followed by the FPPC response letter (dated October 7, 1983). The FPPC response does not contain formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but rather provides guidance by reference. The response suggests the issue involves potential 10% ownership threshold analysis under conflict of interest law.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two Amador County Supervisors who may become involved as directors and owners of a local bank would face conflicts of interest, particularly if a bank stockholder or customer seeks permits or other entitlements from the Board of Supervisors.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC response directs the requester to a previous advice letter (A-82-039) dealing with similar circumstances and suggests seeking specific advice once more details are known, particularly if either supervisor will have 10% or greater ownership interest.

A harder quest it seems to me, however, is posed should either (a) another director or stockholder of the bank or (b) a significant bank customer some entitlement such as a use permit or rezoning the Board of Supervisors. embedding.summary: County Counsel John F. Hahn requests FPPC advice regarding two Amador County Supervisors becoming directors and owners of a local bank and potential conflict of interest issues. The FPPC responds by providing a reference to prior advice letter A-82-039 and recommends obtaining specific advice once additional facts are available.
